title: City of Syracuse cracks down further on apartment slumlords with Nob Hill its latest target
summary: Syracuse Mayor Ben Walsh announced court action against Nob Hill Apartments (managed by Mayfair Management Group) for $340,000 in code violations plus $100/day penalties. The 760-unit complex has deteriorated under current ownership, with residents reporting months without hot water, safety issues, and increased crime. This represents the city's second enforcement action this month against slumlord properties, signaling escalated code enforcement strategy.

political_signal.description: Mayor Walsh is publicly championing aggressive code enforcement against negligent property owners as a policy priority and leadership position. Two enforcement actions filed within weeks demonstrate sustained commitment to resident protection and housing quality standards.

processing_notes: Article references parallel Skyline Apartments case filed earlier in April—pattern suggests systematic enforcement strategy
